experiment: GATE 1c -- realistic API-heavy token->API precision (allowlist ON)
verdict: Identity-scoping + cold-start mitigation: under realistic API-heavy CHURNING load (real operators kube-state-metrics/traefik/local-path/coredns/metrics-server + diverse synthetic clients, restarted throughout), the token->API rule false-fires 3/542 = 0.55% (6 FP/hr), down from 27 (4.98%, 54/hr) before the fix, while the non-allowlisted attacker is detected 12/12. Residual is the hardest cold-start edge.
cold_start_fix: resolve pod SA from live /proc/<pid>/cgroup UID at connect time (lag-free)
residual_fp: 3 unresolved-pod edge cases (process exits before /proc read, or brand-new pod kubectl UID->SA not yet refreshed); 539/542 legit correlation events suppressed
